final_open_lemma.name: KKT barrier uniqueness lemma
final_open_lemma.statement: For w=12,16, the Act-visible shell objective has no non-equal positive interior maximizer; equivalently, any point with shell ratio at or above one is forced to the equal point by the KKT equations.
final_open_lemma.why_not_done: Current evidence is numerical/local/certificate-reduction. A rigorous global barrier or SOS certificate is still missing.
final_open_lemma.why_this_closes: Boundary faces are already controlled; if every positive interior maximizer is equal, then the global maximum is the equal vector and the Talagrand shell inequality follows.

name: Talagrand uniqueness argument state
next_actual_breakthrough_target: Produce a rigorous barrier certificate: high eta implies ratio <= c<1, while ratio near 1 implies negative-definite KKT contraction to the equal point.
what_is_solved: The hidden sector-33/action-return obstruction is closed.
The problem is reduced to the Act-visible shell polynomial.
Small and medium boundary strata are rigorously bounded.
High-support boundary strata are reduced by complement size and strict local maximality.
The naive all-external-field SDPI route is ruled out.
wrong_route_removed: Uniform external-field SDPI with coefficient 2/w is false; proof must be barrier/KKT, not global spectral independence.
